title: Add positional commenting on creative images

requirements.text: Extend the backend CreativeProject data model to include a list of positional comments. Each comment must store: a unique ID, the comment text, x/y position as percentages of the image dimensions, and a timestamp. Implement backend functions to add a comment to a project and delete a comment by ID.

quotes: comments stored per project with x/y position coordinates tied to the image position

acceptanceCriteria: CreativeProject type includes a `comments` field: array of { id: Text, text: Text, x: Float, y: Float, createdAt: Int }
Backend exposes an `addComment(projectId, text, x, y)` function that appends a new comment and returns the updated project
Backend exposes a `deleteComment(projectId, commentId)` function that removes the specified comment
All existing CRUD operations preserve the comments array (empty by default on create)

requirements.text: Add React Query hooks in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` for the two new backend functions: `addComment` (mutation) and `deleteComment` (mutation). Both mutations must invalidate the affected project query on success.
acceptanceCriteria: `useAddComment` mutation hook calls the backend `addComment` and invalidates the project detail query on success
`useDeleteComment` mutation hook calls the backend `deleteComment` and invalidates the project detail query on success

requirements.text: Create a new `ImageWithComments` component in `frontend/src/components/ImageWithComments.tsx`. This component renders a project image and overlays positional comment pins. Clicking anywhere on the image opens an inline text input at the clicked location, allowing the user to type a comment and submit it (drops a pin at that x/y position as a percentage of image width/height). Each pin renders as a small, subtle circular marker. By default, each pin shows a short truncated preview of the comment text. On hover, the full comment text expands into a readable tooltip/popover. Each comment pin includes a delete button (visible on hover) that removes it. The component must handle multiple simultaneous pins cleanly so the image remains readable.

quotes: click anywhere on the image to drop a comment pin at that exact location
Pins appear as subtle markers with a small preview of the comment text by default
Hover to expand — the full comment becomes fully readable on hover
Multiple pins stay subtle until hovered, so the image stays clean
Anyone can delete any comment — no auth needed

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking on the image (not on an existing pin) opens an inline input at that position
Submitting the input calls `useAddComment` with the correct x/y percentages
Pressing Escape or clicking away cancels the new comment input without saving
Each comment pin is visually subtle (small circle marker) when not hovered
Hovering a pin expands it to display the full comment text in a readable tooltip/popover
A delete icon appears on hover; clicking it calls `useDeleteComment` and removes the pin
When multiple pins exist, only the hovered pin is fully expanded; others remain in subtle state
Pin positions are stable across re-renders (stored as percentage of image dimensions)

requirements.text: Integrate the `ImageWithComments` component into `frontend/src/pages/ProjectDetailPage.tsx`. Replace the current static image display with `ImageWithComments` for each project image, passing the project's comments and the add/delete mutation callbacks. The rest of the project detail layout (metadata, edit/delete actions) must remain unchanged.

quotes: Click anywhere on the image in the project detail view to drop a comment pin

acceptanceCriteria: Project detail page renders each project image inside the `ImageWithComments` component
Comments loaded from the project data are displayed as pins on the correct image
Adding and deleting comments updates the UI immediately via query invalidation
All existing project detail functionality (metadata display, edit button, delete button) remains intact

constraints: Do not add any authentication or identity checks — all commenting actions must work without login
Comment pins must be positioned using percentage-based x/y coordinates so they remain accurate at different image display sizes
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
nonGoals: Comment author identity or user names
Real-time comment sync across multiple browser sessions
Commenting on the dashboard thumbnail — only the full image in the detail view
